content:
## MARKET OVERVIEW

1. What is the Current Market Regime?
RISK-ON: Low volatility 23.4% with broad participation; breadth 7/8 sectors positive. Market favors cyclicals and banks as risk appetite dominates.

2. Which sectors are leading?
Banking & Finance and Transportation are leading; Banking ABOVE TREND: YKBNK (1W: +13.86% | 1M: +35.06%) ABOVE TREND, AKBNK (1W: +12.74% | 1M: +31.59%) ABOVE TREND. Transportation shows strength via TAVHL (1W: +8.00% | 1M: +21.32%) ABOVE TREND and THYAO (1W: +6.17% | 1M: +19.25%) ABOVE TREND.

3. Which sectors are lagging?
Technology & Telecom is the lone laggard: KONTR (1W: -8.73% | 1M: -30.92%) BELOW TREND and TTKOM (1W: -1.56% | 1M: +9.83%) CORRECTION. Retail & Food is tepid with SOKM (1W: -1.99% | 1M: +1.13%) CORRECTION and CCOLA (1W: -2.91% | 1M: +0.38%) CORRECTION.

## STOCK PERFORMANCE

4. Top Performing Stocks?
TRALT (1W: +18.25% | 1M: +25.61%) ABOVE TREND, YKBNK (1W: +13.86% | 1M: +35.06%) ABOVE TREND, AKBNK (1W: +12.74% | 1M: +31.59%) ABOVE TREND, TOASO (1W: +10.91% | 1M: +16.95%) ABOVE TREND, EKGYO (1W: +10.20% | 1M: +19.17%) ABOVE TREND. Leadership is concentrated in banks, autos and select chemicals/REITs.

5. Worst Performing Stocks?
KONTR (1W: -8.73% | 1M: -30.92%) BELOW TREND, ALARK (1W: -3.18% | 1M: +14.22%) CORRECTION, TUPRS (1W: -2.95% | 1M: -6.12%) BELOW TREND, CCOLA (1W: -2.91% | 1M: +0.38%) CORRECTION, DOHOL (1W: -2.37% | 1M: -0.65%) BELOW TREND. Weakness reflects stock-specific issues and profit-taking after recent gains.

6. Momentum Stocks?
TRALT (1W: +18.25% | 1M: +25.61%) ABOVE TREND, YKBNK (1W: +13.86% | 1M: +35.06%) ABOVE TREND, AKBNK (1W: +12.74% | 1M: +31.59%) ABOVE TREND, TOASO (1W: +10.91% | 1M: +16.95%) ABOVE TREND, EKGYO (1W: +10.20% | 1M: +19.17%) ABOVE TREND. Momentum concentrated in banks, autos and select energy/chemicals.

## SECTOR OUTLOOK

7. Banking Sector Outlook?
Banking is leading the rally; YKBNK (1W: +13.86% | 1M: +35.06%) ABOVE TREND, AKBNK (1W: +12.74% | 1M: +31.59%) ABOVE TREND, ISCTR (1W: +7.34% | 1M: +20.94%) ABOVE TREND. Continue to prefer large-cap lenders on momentum but watch margins and regulatory headlines.

8. Industrial Sector Outlook?
Heavy industry/autos are constructive: TOASO (1W: +10.91% | 1M: +16.95%) ABOVE TREND, ASELS (1W: +8.42% | 1M: +6.55%) ABOVE TREND, EREGL (1W: +1.68% | 1M: +15.30%) ABOVE TREND. Earnings and export demand remain the key drivers.

9. Energy & Chemicals Outlook?
Mixed: TRALT (1W: +18.25% | 1M: +25.61%) ABOVE TREND and TRMET (1W: +8.08% | 1M: +13.52%) ABOVE TREND lead, while TUPRS (1W: -2.95% | 1M: -6.12%) BELOW TREND lags. Commodity and oil moves will dictate near-term dispersion within the sector.

## PORTFOLIO STRATEGY

10. Portfolio Recommendation?
Risk-on stance: overweight Banking & Finance, select Heavy Industry (autos, defense) and high-quant Retail names; underweight Technology & Telecom and structurally weak Energy names like TUPRS. Trim positions showing stretched short-term momentum and lock gains where exposure is concentrated.

## NEXT WEEK OUTLOOK

11. Stocks to Watch Next Week?
YKBNK (1W: +13.86% | 1M: +35.06%) ABOVE TREND, AKBNK (1W: +12.74% | 1M: +31.59%) ABOVE TREND, TOASO (1W: +10.91% | 1M: +16.95%) ABOVE TREND, TRALT (1W: +18.25% | 1M: +25.61%) ABOVE TREND, TAVHL (1W: +8.00% | 1M: +21.32%) ABOVE TREND. Watch earnings cues, rate guidance and commodity headlines for catalysts.

12. Sectors to Watch Next Week?
Banking & Finance for flow-driven leadership and earnings beats, Heavy Industry/autos for export momentum, and Energy & Chemicals for commodity-triggered moves. Monitor Tech & Telecom for further downside or consolidation.

13. Key Levels & Triggers?
XU100 is at risk-on highs — watch for hold of the breakout and 20/50-day moving averages as support; failure there would signal short-term profit-taking. Key catalysts: domestic CPI and central bank commentary, major bank earnings, oil/commodity swings and global risk sentiment.

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
Risk-on market with low volatility (23.4%) and 7/8 sectors positive; banks and autos lead while tech lags. Opportunity: overweight large banks and cyclicals; risk: rapid profit-taking and sector dispersion if macro or commodity data surprises.
